coll: Quire 1: 1x6+1 (7, fol. 6 single-folio);  Quire 2: 1+1 (9); Quire 3: 1x2 (11); Quire 4: 1x2 (13); Quire 5: 1x4 (17)

layer.label: Sinai Greek NF M 90, Overtext (Greek minuscule)

note: Written in a medium-sized hand, with average to mediocre competence

Fol. 15 was originally a bifolio from a much smaller Ethiopian book, now turned at 90 degrees to form an individual folio in the Greek codex. The gutter fold of the original bifolio is still visible. Because of the wear in the center of the Ethiopian folio, it is not possible to tell if there were originally two or four columns. Only the outer edges of the text block are discernible. The original size of the bifolio suggests that this was only one column

note.value: Description by Steve Delamarter, Getatchew Haile

Clearly from a different book than the other two UTOs in this codex: there are differences in layout from the UTO Unidentified Homily; and differences in the size of the hand, the ductus, and the lack of rubrication in relation to the UTO Unidentified Horologium
The surviving text is insufficient to identify genre, let alone specific content
Description of undertext by Steve Delamarter, Getatchew Haile

note: Each column has about seven letters, thus, with 24 lines, each column could hold around 168 letters

text_unit.label: Unidentified Homily on themes from Deuteronomy 6:5, Matthew 22:37, Deuteronomy 20:2, Matthew 19:21

work_wit.alt_title: Homily on themes from Deuteronomy 6:5, Matthew 22:37, Deuteronomy 20:2, Matthew 19:21

note.value: There are no other known attestations of this text. This may well be a text of John Chrysostom, previously unattested in Ethiopic
Description by Steve Delamarter, Getatchew Haile
Clearly from a different book than the other two UTOs in this codex; layout, size of letters, and ductus are all distinct
Description of undertext by Steve Delamarter, Getatchew Haile

note: Written in a medium-sized hand with average competence

The folios from this object were originally independent bifolios sewn together from a much smaller Ethiopian book to form a bifolio for the new Greek book, oriented at 90 degrees to the overtext. The two bifolios were from an Ethiopian book whose size was approximately half the size of the new Greek book, thus when sewn together and trimmed, they provided a sheet of the right size and shape for the new Greek book. The seam is now in the gutter of the Greek book in its current shape. The gutter folds of the original Ethiopic sheets are still visible, oriented at 90 degrees to the orientation of the current Greek codex

note.value: There are eight occurrences in the text of rubricated headings for the hours, indicating both the hour and the text to be read (e.g., Gospel, Acts) | There are no earlier attestations of this Horologion text
Description by Steve Delamarter, Getatchew Haile

Several features like script size, layout, folio size, etc., make it clear that this UTO is quite unrelated to the UTO Unidentified Homily. Though the layout may be similar to the UTO Unidentified Text, the hand is different
Description of undertext by Steve Delamarter, Getatchew Haile

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

note.value: Foliation in pencil provisional, not sequential
Unsewn quires, one small fragment; folios partially fragmentary and badly damaged | Text may be missing
